sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
Embedded search adds semantic (meaning-based) search to cortex alongside the existing FTS5 keyword search. The system uses local embeddings via the `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` model (384 dimensions) stored in a sqlite-vec virtual table within `cortex.db`.

### Components

**Embedding module (`cortex/index/embeddings.py`)** — encapsulates model loading and text-to-vector conversion. Supports multiple backends with priority fallback: fastembed (ONNX Runtime) > sentence-transformers (PyTorch) > deterministic hash fallback. Model is lazily loaded on first semantic query to avoid blocking MCP startup.

**DB layer (`cortex/index/db.py`)** — adds `node_embeddings` sqlite-vec virtual table keyed by node ID with a content hash column. Provides `upsert_embedding()` (skip unchanged via hash), `semantic_search()` (nearest-neighbor lookup), and `init_embeddings()` (table creation with graceful degradation if sqlite-vec unavailable). Doc sections are also added to the existing `node_fts` FTS5 table.

**Builder integration (`cortex/index/builder.py`)** — two new build steps: (1) index doc sections into FTS5 (heading + content), (2) generate embeddings for all code nodes (level_1 + level_2) and doc sections (heading + content) with batch processing and content-hash skip.

**MCP tool (`cortex/mcp_server.py`)** — `cortex_search` gains two parameters: `mode` (keyword|semantic, default: keyword) selects search strategy; `scope` (code|docs|all, default: all) filters result types. Semantic mode embeds the query and returns nearest neighbors from sqlite-vec. Existing FTS behavior is completely unchanged when mode=keyword.

sections.heading: Decision Log
sections.content:
| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Embedding model | all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384 dims, ~80MB) | Good quality for code/docs, small footprint, well-supported |
| Primary backend | fastembed (ONNX Runtime) | Cross-platform compatibility; PyTorch has DLL issues on Windows |
| Vector storage | sqlite-vec virtual table in cortex.db | Single DB file, no external dependencies, pure C extension |
| Search integration | Unified `cortex_search` with mode/scope params | No new tools; agents use familiar interface |
| Default mode | keyword (FTS5) | Backward compatible; semantic opt-in |
| Doc section FTS | Added to node_fts alongside code nodes | Closes pre-existing gap where 635 doc sections were unsearchable |
| Embedding skip | Content hash comparison | Avoid redundant recomputation during build |
| Model loading | Lazy on first semantic query | Don't block MCP server startup |
| Graceful degradation | Fall back to FTS when sqlite-vec unavailable | Feature is optional, not a hard dependency |

sections.heading: Dependencies
sections.content:
**Runtime (optional, via `[semantic]` extra):**
- `fastembed` — ONNX Runtime-based embedding, primary backend
- `onnxruntime` >=1.17,<1.20 — pinned for Windows compatibility

**Runtime (optional, via `[semantic-torch]` extra):**
- `sentence-transformers` — PyTorch-based alternative backend

**Runtime (bundled):**
- `sqlite-vec` — SQLite extension for vector similarity search

All semantic search dependencies are optional. Without them, cortex degrades gracefully to FTS-only search.
